@/Users/canerden/Downloads/Nieuwe opname 3.m4a so we need to transcribe the records of a negotiation challenge with my friend. 2 speakers. how we can transcribe it perfectly? 
then will upload to this website; https://www.ainegotiationlab-vanderbilt.com/model_aslani/
for context: Model 1 uses the coding scheme of Aslani, Ramirez-Martin, Brett, Yao, Semnani-Azada, Zhang, Tinsley, Weingart, and Adair (2014), “Measuring negotiation strategy and predicting outcomes: Self-reports, behavioral codes, and linguistic codes,” presented at the annual conference of the International Association for Conflict Management, Leiden, The Netherlands.1 Click here for full paper
As the authors describe in the 2014 paper, “we developed a 14-item code based on prior negotiation coding schemes (e.g. Adair & Brett, 2005; Gunia et al., 2011; Weingart et al., 1990)2 to measure participants’ use of tactics. Major categories in the code were information, offers, substantiation, negative and positive reactions, and a miscellaneous category.” The simulation used in their study was The Sweet Shop (newnegotiationexercises.com). The authors shared with us 75 transcripts they had collected and coded using human coders.
Each of the codes is shown below except for “Response Embarrassed” which appeared only once across all transcripts, making it hard to teach that code to the model. For each of the remaining 13 codes, we provide a definition of the code, a short explanation, and sample sentences from the transcripts. The sample sentences let you see how these scholars operationalized their codes, which is what Model 1 learned from and tries to reproduce when coding your transcripts. As with any coding scheme, different scholars might operationalize concepts slightly differently. You should decide if this coding scheme will be useful to you by reviewing how the authors used it.
When reporting your results from this model, please cite this paper:
Friedman, R., Brett, J., Cho, J., Zhan, X., et al, 2024, Coding negotiations with AI: Instructions and validation for coding model 1, /static/assets/VandAI_Neg_Lab_Model1_Paper.v2.pdf.
To submit your transcript for the model to code, drag and drop one or several transcript files into the section below. If you see the files you want to code listed properly (just below the “Upload” button), click submit. Note that each time you upload new files, those will replace the previously uploaded files, and be ready to submit
Once your files are successfully uploaded, you will see a Task ID and Passcode. Be sure to save those, you cannot access them later! It will take about 3-15 minutes for Claude to process each transcript, depending on how many users Claude has at that moment. When you think it might be finished, go to the Results Retrieval Page and enter your Task ID and Passcode. If the results are ready, they will download automatically.
If you need to manually retrieve your results, you can use the Results Retrieval Page with your Task ID and Passcode.
We suggest submitting just a few files at a time, so that you can check the output before doing too many analyses. The output file will include:
Transcript Name
Speaker
The text (thought unit, sentence, or speaking turn)
The code assigned to that text
Consistency score for that code
Upload or Drag Files 
1 This project was later expanded and published (but did not use the coding) as Aslani, S., Ramirez‐Marin, J., Brett, J., Yao, J., Semnani‐Azad, Z., Zhang, Z. X., ... & Adair, W. (2016). Dignity, face, and honor cultures: A study of negotiation strategy and outcomes in three cultures. Journal of Organizational Behavior, 37(8), 1178-1201.

"""
Transcribe a 2-speaker negotiation recording using AssemblyAI.
Output is formatted for the Vanderbilt AI Negotiation Lab (ainegotiationlab-vanderbilt.com).

Usage:
    python3 transcribe_negotiation.py

Requirements:
    pip3 install assemblyai
"""

import assemblyai as aai
import os

# ── CONFIGURE THESE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
API_KEY   = "YOUR_ASSEMBLYAI_API_KEY_HERE"   # get free at assemblyai.com
AUDIO_FILE = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/Nieuwe opname 3.m4a"
OUTPUT_FILE = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/negotiation_transcript.txt"
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

aai.settings.api_key = API_KEY

config = aai.TranscriptionConfig(
    speaker_labels=True,        # diarize into Speaker A / Speaker B
    speakers_expected=2,        # tell it exactly 2 speakers
    language_detection=True,    # auto-detect language (handles Dutch/English)
)

print(f"Uploading and transcribing: {os.path.basename(AUDIO_FILE)}")
print("This may take 2–5 minutes for a typical negotiation recording...\n")

transcript = aai.Transcriber().transcribe(AUDIO_FILE, config)

if transcript.status == aai.TranscriptStatus.error:
    print(f"Error: {transcript.error}")
    exit(1)

# ── Build output ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lines = []
current_speaker = None
current_text = []

for utterance in transcript.utterances:
    speaker = f"Speaker {utterance.speaker}"   # "Speaker A" or "Speaker B"
    text = utterance.text.strip()

    # Merge consecutive turns by the same speaker
    if speaker == current_speaker:
        current_text.append(text)
    else:
        if current_speaker is not None:
            lines.append(f"{current_speaker}: {' '.join(current_text)}")
        current_speaker = speaker
        current_text = [text]

# flush last speaker
if current_speaker:
    lines.append(f"{current_speaker}: {' '.join(current_text)}")

output = "\n".join(lines)

with open(OUTPUT_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write(output)

print(f"✓ Transcript saved to: {OUTPUT_FILE}")
print(f"  Lines: {len(lines)}")
print("\n── Preview (first 10 lines) ──")
for line in lines[:10]:
    print(line)

**Steps to run:**

1. **Get your free AssemblyAI API key** at [assemblyai.com](https://www.assemblyai.com) → Dashboard → API Keys

2. **Edit the script** — open `/Users/canerden/Downloads/transcribe_negotiation.py` and replace `YOUR_ASSEMBLYAI_API_KEY_HERE` with your key

3. **Run it:**
   ```bash
   python3 /Users/canerden/Downloads/transcribe_negotiation.py
   ```

4. **Result:** `negotiation_transcript.txt` in your Downloads folder, formatted like:
   ```
   Speaker A: I was thinking we could start at 500 boxes.
   Speaker B: That seems high, what's your reasoning?
   ```

---

## Important notes for the Vanderbilt upload

- The website accepts `.txt` files with speaker-labeled turns
- `speakers_expected=2` ensures AssemblyAI won't fragment into 3+ phantom speakers
- `language_detection=True` handles it whether the negotiation was in English or Dutch
- The output format (one turn per line, `Speaker X: text`) matches what the coding model expects

---

## Alternative if you don't want to sign up for an API

Let me know and I can set up a **local Whisper + pyannote** pipeline instead — no API key needed, runs fully on your Mac, but takes ~10 min to install models.

"""
Transcribe a 2-speaker negotiation recording using AssemblyAI.
Output is formatted for the Vanderbilt AI Negotiation Lab (ainegotiationlab-vanderbilt.com).

Usage:
    python3 transcribe_negotiation.py

Requirements:
    pip3 install assemblyai
"""

import assemblyai as aai
import os

# ── CONFIGURE THESE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
API_KEY   = "fa02d8e17b15441d86a89229e5e87515"
AUDIO_FILE = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/Nieuwe opname 3.m4a"
OUTPUT_FILE = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/negotiation_transcript.txt"
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

aai.settings.api_key = API_KEY

config = aai.TranscriptionConfig(
    speaker_labels=True,        # diarize into Speaker A / Speaker B
    speakers_expected=2,        # tell it exactly 2 speakers
    language_detection=True,    # auto-detect language (handles Dutch/English)
)

print(f"Uploading and transcribing: {os.path.basename(AUDIO_FILE)}")
print("This may take 2–5 minutes for a typical negotiation recording...\n")

transcript = aai.Transcriber().transcribe(AUDIO_FILE, config)

if transcript.status == aai.TranscriptStatus.error:
    print(f"Error: {transcript.error}")
    exit(1)

# ── Build output ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lines = []
current_speaker = None
current_text = []

for utterance in transcript.utterances:
    speaker = f"Speaker {utterance.speaker}"   # "Speaker A" or "Speaker B"
    text = utterance.text.strip()

    # Merge consecutive turns by the same speaker
    if speaker == current_speaker:
        current_text.append(text)
    else:
        if current_speaker is not None:
            lines.append(f"{current_speaker}: {' '.join(current_text)}")
        current_speaker = speaker
        current_text = [text]

# flush last speaker
if current_speaker:
    lines.append(f"{current_speaker}: {' '.join(current_text)}")

output = "\n".join(lines)

with open(OUTPUT_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write(output)

print(f"✓ Transcript saved to: {OUTPUT_FILE}")
print(f"  Lines: {len(lines)}")
print("\n── Preview (first 10 lines) ──")
for line in lines[:10]:
    print(line)

# Transcript File Format Requirements

## Expected Format

The system requires **Excel files** for transcript uploads. The documentation specifies: "Set up your transcripts for analysis by putting them into an excel sheet."

## File Specifications

- **Maximum size**: 999 rows per file (longer transcripts must be split)
- **Required columns**: 
  - "SpeakerName" (no space in heading)
  - "Content" 
  - "ResearcherName" (no space in heading)
  - "Email"
  - "Institution"

## Unit of Analysis Options

Transcripts can be organized using one of three approaches:
- Speaking turns (speakers alternate)
- Sentences (identified by `.?!` punctuation)
- Thought units (requires careful analysis)

The documentation notes that "speaking turns" provides the closest alignment with the training data, particularly since the original research used this method.

## Naming Convention

Files must follow this pattern: `YourName_StudyName_1`, `YourName_StudyName_2`, etc.

**Note**: While an Excel template download is referenced, the actual file isn't embedded in the provided content. However, complete formatting examples showing both speaking turn and sentence-based layouts are illustrated in the documentation.

input.content:
"""
Convert negotiation_transcript.txt → Vanderbilt AI Negotiation Lab Excel format.

Required columns: SpeakerName | Content | ResearcherName | Email | Institution
File naming: YourName_StudyName_1.xlsx
Max 999 rows per file.
"""

import openpyxl
import os

# ── CONFIGURE THESE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRANSCRIPT_FILE = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/negotiation_transcript.txt"
OUTPUT_DIR      = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/"

RESEARCHER_NAME = "CanerDen"           # no spaces
EMAIL           = "caner.den@vlerick.com"   # your email
INSTITUTION     = "Vlerick Business School"
STUDY_NAME      = "NegotiationChallenge"    # no spaces
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Read transcript
rows = []
with open(TRANSCRIPT_FILE, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        if ": " in line:
            speaker, content = line.split(": ", 1)
            rows.append((speaker.strip(), content.strip()))
        else:
            print(f"  Skipping unparseable line: {line[:60]}")

print(f"Total speaking turns: {len(rows)}")

"""
Transcribe a 2-speaker negotiation recording using AssemblyAI REST API.
Output is formatted for the Vanderbilt AI Negotiation Lab (ainegotiationlab-vanderbilt.com).

Usage:
    python3 transcribe_negotiation.py
"""

import requests
import time
import os

# ── CONFIGURE THESE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
API_KEY     = "fa02d8e17b15441d86a89229e5e87515"
AUDIO_FILE  = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/Nieuwe opname 3.m4a"
OUTPUT_FILE = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/negotiation_transcript.txt"
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

BASE = "https://api.assemblyai.com"
HEADERS = {"authorization": API_KEY}

# 1. Upload the audio file
print(f"Uploading: {os.path.basename(AUDIO_FILE)} ({os.path.getsize(AUDIO_FILE)/1e6:.1f} MB)...")
with open(AUDIO_FILE, "rb") as f:
    upload_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/v2/upload", headers=HEADERS, data=f)
upload_resp.raise_for_status()
audio_url = upload_resp.json()["upload_url"]
print(f"  Uploaded → {audio_url[:60]}...")

# 2. Submit transcription job
payload = {
    "audio_url": audio_url,
    "speaker_labels": True,
    "speakers_expected": 2,
    "language_detection": True,
    "speech_models": ["universal-2"],   # current API field name
}
job_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/v2/transcript", json=payload, headers=HEADERS)
if not job_resp.ok:
    print(f"Error response: {job_resp.status_code} {job_resp.text}")
    exit(1)
transcript_id = job_resp.json()["id"]
print(f"  Job submitted → ID: {transcript_id}")

# 3. Poll until done
print("Waiting for transcription", end="", flush=True)
while True:
    poll = requests.get(f"{BASE}/v2/transcript/{transcript_id}", headers=HEADERS)
    poll.raise_for_status()
    data = poll.json()
    status = data["status"]
    if status == "completed":
        print(" done!")
        break
    elif status == "error":
        print(f"\nError: {data['error']}")
        exit(1)
    else:
        print(".", end="", flush=True)
        time.sleep(5)

# 4. Build speaker-labelled output
utterances = data.get("utterances") or []
lines = []
current_speaker = None
current_parts = []

for utt in utterances:
    speaker = f"Speaker {utt['speaker']}"
    text = utt["text"].strip()
    if speaker == current_speaker:
        current_parts.append(text)
    else:
        if current_speaker:
            lines.append(f"{current_speaker}: {' '.join(current_parts)}")
        current_speaker = speaker
        current_parts = [text]

if current_speaker:
    lines.append(f"{current_speaker}: {' '.join(current_parts)}")

with open(OUTPUT_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("\n".join(lines))

print(f"\n✓ Transcript saved to: {OUTPUT_FILE}")
print(f"  Total turns: {len(lines)}")
print("\n── Preview (first 10 lines) ──")
for line in lines[:10]:
    print(line)

"""
Convert negotiation_transcript.txt → Vanderbilt AI Negotiation Lab Excel format.

Required columns: SpeakerName | Content | ResearcherName | Email | Institution
File naming: YourName_StudyName_1.xlsx
Max 999 rows per file.
"""

import openpyxl
import os

# ── CONFIGURE THESE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRANSCRIPT_FILE = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/negotiation_transcript.txt"
OUTPUT_DIR      = "/Users/canerden/Downloads/"

RESEARCHER_NAME = "CanErden"
EMAIL           = "can.erden@edu.vlerick.com"
INSTITUTION     = "Vlerick Business School"
STUDY_NAME      = "SweetShop"
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Speaker label mapping (from transcript → role name)
# Speaker A = ice cream shop, Speaker B = bakery shop (identified from transcript)
SPEAKER_MAP = {
    "Speaker A": "IceCreamShop",
    "Speaker B": "BakeryShop",
}

# Read transcript
rows = []
with open(TRANSCRIPT_FILE, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        if ": " in line:
            raw_speaker, content = line.split(": ", 1)
            speaker = SPEAKER_MAP.get(raw_speaker.strip(), raw_speaker.strip())
            rows.append((speaker, content.strip()))
        else:
            print(f"  Skipping unparseable line: {line[:60]}")

print(f"Total speaking turns: {len(rows)}")

# Split into chunks of 999 (website limit)
CHUNK = 999
chunks = [rows[i:i+CHUNK] for i in range(0, len(rows), CHUNK)]

saved_files = []
for idx, chunk in enumerate(chunks, start=1):
    filename = f"{RESEARCHER_NAME}_{STUDY_NAME}_{idx}.xlsx"
    filepath = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename)

    wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
    ws = wb.active
    ws.title = "Transcript"

    # Header row (exact names required by Vanderbilt — no spaces in SpeakerName/ResearcherName)
    ws.append(["SpeakerName", "Content", "ResearcherName", "Email", "Institution"])

    for i, (speaker, content) in enumerate(chunk):
        # Researcher info only in the first data row
        if i == 0:
            ws.append([speaker, content, RESEARCHER_NAME, EMAIL, INSTITUTION])
        else:
            ws.append([speaker, content, "", "", ""])

    # Auto-width for readability
    for col in ws.columns:
        max_len = max((len(str(cell.value or "")) for cell in col), default=0)
        ws.column_dimensions[col[0].column_letter].width = min(max_len + 2, 80)

    wb.save(filepath)
    saved_files.append(filepath)
    print(f"✓ Saved: {filename}  ({len(chunk)} rows)")

print(f"\nDone! {len(saved_files)} file(s) ready to upload to Vanderbilt.")
print("Upload at: https://www.ainegotiationlab-vanderbilt.com/model_aslani/")
